item.text: I’ll first profile the test variants and the local environment, then build a leakage-safe annotation pipeline. The strongest practical route is likely a calibrated ensemble of precomputed public pathogenicity/conservation scores, with consequence-aware rules and test-tuple exclusion documented in the manifest.

item.text: I can make the clinical calibration genuinely leakage-safe rather than hand-tuning it: a public ClinVar-VEP table contains CADD, phyloP, consequence, and labels. I’ll remove every exact test tuple first (2,037 overlaps), fit only on the remaining rows, and validate chromosome-held-out. The test-time features come independently from VEP; no test labels or ClinVar significance fields will enter the model.

item.text: A partial-fit audit exposed one predictor I won’t use: MetaRNN nearly reconstructs the older ClinVar labels, so its historical supervised-training membership is too uncertain for this task’s leakage standard. I’m excluding it from the model. Population frequency remains because it is an independent observation, not a clinical label; current ClinVar significance fields are still ignored.

The service is checkpoint-safe but the test queue has slowed behind the larger training queue. I’m repartitioning only the unfinished half into a fresh worker pool; completed batches stay untouched, and final annotations are deduplicated by exact tuple.
